_comment: Identity layer catalogue — deterministic gap analysis controls for auth methods, break glass, Entra hardening, PIM, and IGA. Transcribed from Deploy-*.ps1 scripts.

controls.title: Disable SMS Authentication
controls.description: SMS OTP is phishable and should be disabled in favour of FIDO2 or Microsoft Authenticator. CISA recommends disabling SMS-based MFA.

framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.3 Require MFA for Externally-Exposed Applications
framework_controls.cisa: CISA Phishing-Resistant MFA guidance — deprecate SMS OTP

controls.title: Disable Voice Call Authentication
controls.description: Voice call OTP is phishable and should be disabled in favour of phishing-resistant methods.

framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.3 Require MFA for Externally-Exposed Applications
framework_controls.cisa: CISA Phishing-Resistant MFA guidance — deprecate voice OTP

controls.title: Enable Microsoft Authenticator
controls.description: Microsoft Authenticator with number matching and location display should be enabled as the primary MFA method.

framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.3 Require MFA for Externally-Exposed Applications
framework_controls.cisa: CISA MFA guidance — push notification with number matching

controls.title: Enable FIDO2 Security Keys
controls.description: FIDO2/passkey support should be enabled to provide phishing-resistant MFA capability.

framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.5 Require MFA for Administrative Access
framework_controls.cisa: CISA Phishing-Resistant MFA — FIDO2 recommended

controls.title: Enable Passkey Support
controls.description: Passkey (platform authenticator) support should be enabled alongside FIDO2 security keys.

framework_controls.cisa: CISA Phishing-Resistant MFA — passkey recommended

controls.title: Enable Temporary Access Pass
controls.description: TAP should be enabled to allow secure onboarding of passwordless credentials (FIDO2, Authenticator).

framework_controls.cisa: CISA passwordless onboarding guidance

controls.title: Disable Software OATH Tokens
controls.description: Software OATH tokens (third-party TOTP apps) should be disabled when Microsoft Authenticator is the standard.

framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.3 Require MFA for Externally-Exposed Applications

controls.title: No Weak Authentication Methods Enabled
controls.description: No weak methods (SMS, voice, email OTP) should remain enabled. This is a composite check across all weak method types.

framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.3/6.5 MFA requirements
framework_controls.cisa: CISA Phishing-Resistant MFA — eliminate phishable factors

break_glass.name: Break Glass Accounts
break_glass.description: Emergency access account configuration from Deploy-BreakGlass.ps1

controls.title: Break Glass Accounts Exist
controls.description: At least 2 emergency access accounts should exist for tenant recovery scenarios.

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(c) — business continuity and disaster recovery
framework_controls.iso27001: A.5.30 ICT Readiness for Business Continuity
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 5.1 Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Accounts
framework_controls.cisa: CISA Emergency Access Accounts guidance

controls.title: Break Glass Accounts Enabled
controls.description: All discovered break glass accounts must be enabled (not disabled).

framework_controls.iso27001: A.5.30 ICT Readiness for Business Continuity
framework_controls.cisa: CISA Emergency Access — accounts must be functional

controls.title: Break Glass Accounts Are Cloud-Only
controls.description: Emergency access accounts must be cloud-only (not synced from on-premises AD) to ensure availability during hybrid identity failures.

framework_controls.iso27001: A.5.30 ICT Readiness for Business Continuity
framework_controls.cisa: CISA Emergency Access — cloud-only requirement

controls.title: Break Glass Accounts Have Global Admin Role
controls.description: Emergency access accounts must hold the Global Administrator role for full recovery capability.

framework_controls.iso27001: A.5.30 ICT Readiness for Business Continuity
framework_controls.cisa: CISA Emergency Access — Global Admin required

controls.title: Break Glass Accounts In CA Exclusion Group
controls.description: Emergency access accounts must be members of the CA exclusion group to bypass Conditional Access during emergencies.

framework_controls.iso27001: A.5.30 ICT Readiness for Business Continuity
framework_controls.cisa: CISA Emergency Access — CA exclusion required

controls.title: Break Glass Accounts Not MFA Registered
controls.description: Emergency access accounts should NOT have MFA registered — they rely on strong passwords and CA exclusion, ensuring access even when MFA infrastructure is unavailable.

framework_controls.iso27001: A.5.30 ICT Readiness for Business Continuity
framework_controls.cisa: CISA Emergency Access — MFA independence

controls.title: Break Glass Accounts Not At Risk
controls.description: Emergency access accounts should have no active risk detections in Entra ID Protection.

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(a) — risk assessment and incident handling
framework_controls.cisa: CISA Emergency Access — monitor for compromise

controls.title: CA Exclusion Group Exists
controls.description: The dedicated CA exclusion group for break glass accounts must exist.

framework_controls.iso27001: A.5.30 ICT Readiness for Business Continuity

entra_hardening.name: Entra ID Tenant Hardening
entra_hardening.description: Tenant security hardening settings from Deploy-EntraHardening.ps1

controls.title: Restrict App Registration to Admins
controls.description: Non-admin users should not be allowed to register applications. Uncontrolled app registrations expand the attack surface.

framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.2 Establish an Access Revoking Process
framework_controls.cisa: CISA Entra ID hardening — restrict app registrations

controls.title: Restrict Tenant Creation to Admins
controls.description: Non-admin users should not be allowed to create new Azure AD tenants to prevent shadow IT.

framework_controls.cisa: CISA Entra ID hardening — restrict tenant creation

controls.title: Restrict User Consent to Apps
controls.description: User consent to applications should be restricted. Admin consent workflow should be required to prevent OAuth consent phishing.

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — access control and consent management
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.8 Define and Maintain Role-Based Access Control
framework_controls.cisa: CISA OAuth consent phishing — restrict user consent

controls.title: Disable LinkedIn Integration
controls.description: LinkedIn account connection should be disabled to prevent data leakage to external social platform.

framework_controls.cis18: CIS 3.3 Configure Data Access Control Lists

controls.title: Restrict Guest Invitations to Admins
controls.description: Guest invitations should be restricted to admins and users with the Guest Inviter role. Open guest invite policies create external access risk.

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — access control for external identities
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.8 Define and Maintain Role-Based Access Control
framework_controls.cisa: CISA external identity — restrict guest invitations

controls.title: Restrict Admin Portal Access
controls.description: Non-admin users should be restricted from accessing the Entra ID admin portal.

framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.5 Require MFA for Administrative Access

controls.title: Security Defaults Disabled (CA In Use)
controls.description: Security Defaults must be disabled when Conditional Access policies are deployed. Having both active causes conflicts.

framework_controls.cis18: CIS 4.1 Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process

controls.title: Risk-Based CA Policies Deployed
controls.description: Risk-based Conditional Access policies (user risk and sign-in risk) must be present. These replace the legacy Identity Protection policies.

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(a) — risk assessment and incident handling
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.3 Require MFA for Externally-Exposed Applications
framework_controls.cisa: CISA risk-based access — sign-in and user risk CA policies

pim.name: Privileged Identity Management
pim.description: PIM best-practice controls. Every Global Admin must be PIM-eligible. Break-glass accounts are the only exception. Activation policies enforce MFA, justification, and approval for critical roles.

controls.title: No Standing Global Administrator Assignments
controls.description: Every Global Admin must be PIM-eligible, not permanently active. Maximum 2 Global Admins total. Break-glass accounts are the only exception and must be documented and monitored.

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — privileged access management; standing GA is the highest-risk identity misconfiguration
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.2 Privileged Access Rights — no permanent privileged assignments
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.5 Require MFA for Administrative Access; CIS 5.4 Restrict Administrator Privileges
framework_controls.cisa: CISA privileged access — JIT for Global Admins; zero standing access
framework_controls.microsoft_baseline: Microsoft Identity Secure Score: Use PIM for Global Admin

controls.title: Activation Duration Capped by Role Sensitivity
controls.description: Global Admin, Privileged Role Admin, Privileged Authentication Admin: max 4 hours. Security Admin, Exchange Admin, SharePoint Admin, Intune Admin and similar operational roles: max 8 hours. Prevents extended sessions that expand the attack window.

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — session management and privileged access time limits
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.2 Privileged Access Rights — time-bound access controls
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.5 Require MFA for Administrative Access
framework_controls.cisa: CISA privileged access — limit activation window

controls.title: Approval Workflow Required for Critical Roles
controls.description: Roles requiring approval: Global Admin, Privileged Role Admin, Privileged Authentication Admin. Operational roles (Security Admin, Exchange Admin, SharePoint Admin, Intune Admin) require MFA + justification only, NOT approval — to avoid operational friction.

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — approval workflow for highest-privilege access
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.2 Privileged Access Rights — dual-control for critical roles
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.5 Require MFA for Administrative Access
framework_controls.cisa: CISA PIM — approval required for Global Admin activation

controls.title: MFA Required on Every Activation
controls.description: Every PIM role activation must require MFA re-authentication at activation time. Phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2 or WHfB) required specifically for Global Admin, Privileged Role Admin, Privileged Authentication Admin.

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — multi-factor authentication on privileged activation
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.2 Privileged Access Rights; A.8.5 Secure Authentication — MFA at activation
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.5 Require MFA for Administrative Access — at activation time, not just sign-in
framework_controls.cisa: CISA Phishing-Resistant MFA — required for privileged role activation

controls.title: Justification Required on Activation
controls.description: Every activation must require written justification. Feeds audit trails for compliance. Relevant to ISO 27001 Annex A 8.2 and NIS2 accountability requirements.

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — accountability and audit trail for privileged access
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.2 Privileged Access Rights — justification and audit logging
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 8.5 Collect Detailed Audit Logs

controls.title: No Synced Accounts in Privileged Roles
controls.description: On-prem synced accounts (those with onPremisesImmutableId set) must not hold Global Admin, Privileged Role Admin, or Privileged Authentication Admin. On-prem compromise directly escalates to cloud admin in hybrid environments.

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(a) — risk assessment; on-prem compromise path to cloud
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.2 Privileged Access Rights — cloud-only requirement for critical roles
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.5 Require MFA for Administrative Access; CIS 12.2 Establish and Maintain Secure Network Architecture
framework_controls.cisa: CISA hybrid identity — synced accounts must not hold cloud admin roles
framework_controls.microsoft_baseline: Microsoft recommended: cloud-only accounts for privileged Entra roles

controls.title: Access Reviews on Eligible Assignments
controls.description: PIM eligible assignments must have recurring access review configured. Critical roles (Global Admin, Privileged Role Admin, Privileged Auth Admin): minimum quarterly review. Operational roles: minimum semi-annual review.

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — periodic access review for privileged roles
framework_controls.iso27001: A.5.18 Access Rights — periodic access certification
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.2 Establish an Access Revoking Process
framework_controls.cisa: CISA access governance — periodic access reviews for privileged roles

controls.title: Access Reviews Configured
controls.description: Access review campaigns should be configured for periodic access certification of privileged roles, guests, and sensitive groups.

framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.2 Establish an Access Revoking Process
framework_controls.cisa: CISA access governance — periodic access reviews

controls.title: No Overdue Access Reviews
controls.description: All access review instances should be completed on time. Overdue reviews indicate governance gaps.

framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.2 Establish an Access Revoking Process

controls.title: Access Review Count Sufficient
controls.description: Multiple access review series should exist covering privileged roles, guests, and sensitive groups.

controls.title: Lifecycle Workflows Configured
controls.description: Lifecycle workflows (joiner/mover/leaver) should be configured for automated identity lifecycle management.

framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.1 Establish an Access Granting Process
framework_controls.cisa: CISA identity lifecycle — joiner/mover/leaver automation

controls.title: Lifecycle Workflow Count Sufficient
controls.description: At least 3 lifecycle workflows should exist covering joiner, mover, and leaver scenarios.

controls.title: Lifecycle Workflows Enabled
controls.description: Configured lifecycle workflows should be enabled, not just created.

framework_controls.cisa: CISA identity lifecycle — workflows must be active

controls.title: Entitlement Management Configured
controls.description: At least one access package should exist in entitlement management for self-service access requests.

framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.1 Establish an Access Granting Process

controls.title: No Pending Access Requests Backlog
controls.description: Pending entitlement management requests should be zero or minimal, indicating timely processing.

framework_controls.iso27001: A.5.18 Access Rights — timely access provisioning
